guideline_source: American Diabetes Association (ADA) Standards of Care 2024
overview: Dietary management of Type 2 Diabetes focuses on controlling blood glucose, reducing cardiovascular risk, and achieving a healthy body weight through carbohydrate quality and quantity management.
meal_pattern: 3 structured meals + 1-2 snacks per day; consistent carbohydrate distribution; do not skip meals
special_notes: Mediterranean and DASH diets both show strong evidence for diabetes management. Monitor blood glucose 2 hours after meals.

overview: A heart-healthy diet reduces LDL cholesterol, triglycerides, blood pressure, and inflammation. Emphasizes plant-based foods, healthy fats, and limits saturated and trans fats.

guideline_source: DASH Diet — National Heart, Lung, and Blood Institute (NHLBI)
overview: The DASH (Dietary Approaches to Stop Hypertension) diet is the gold standard for managing high blood pressure through dietary means.

guideline_source: KDOQI Clinical Practice Guidelines for Nutrition in CKD 2020
overview: CKD diet focuses on protecting kidney function by limiting nutrients that accumulate in the blood when kidneys are impaired. Protein restriction may slow progression.
limit_foods: high-potassium foods (bananas, potatoes, tomatoes — leach potatoes)

guideline_source: WHO & NICE Guidelines on Obesity Management
overview: Sustainable weight loss requires a moderate caloric deficit combined with high satiety foods, regular eating patterns, and behavior change support.
avoid_foods: skip skipping meals — leads to overeating
meal_pattern: 3 regular meals; avoid snacking; mindful eating practices
